original: The 2024 Tokyo gubernatorial election took place on 7 July 2024 to elect the Governor of Tokyo. Incumbent Yuriko Koike was re-elected for a third term, albeit at a reduced share of the votes compared to her 2016 election and 2020 re-election. Shinji Ishimaru, the former Mayor of Akitakata, exceeded expectations to place second behind Koike. paraphrase: On July 7, 2024, the election for the Governor of Tokyo was held. The current officeholder, Yuriko Koike, secured another term, her third overall, though her percentage of the vote was lower than in her 2016 victory and her 2020 re-election campaign. Shinji Ishimaru, who previously served as the Mayor of Akitakata, surpassed predictions by finishing in second place, trailing only Koike. In contrast, Renhō, a sitting lawmaker who received backing from the primary left-leaning opposition parties and was widely regarded as Koike's principal challenger before the election, performed substantially worse than her poll numbers suggested, ending up in third position. Ishimaru's unexpectedly strong showing is attributed to his widespread utilization of social media channels for his campaign, which helped him gain support from independent voters and younger demographics. Conversely, Renhō's weak performance is believed to be connected to her association with the Japanese Communist Party (JCP), which reportedly led to a decline in support among certain voter segments.

original: The Battle of Kirkuk took place in the city of Kirkuk in northern Iraq between Iraqi Kurdistan and the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant. On the night of January 29, around 150 ISIL fighters attacked positions south and west of the city of Kirkuk, Iraq, which were temporarily under the control of the Peshmerga. The ISIL offensive began under the cover of dense fog and succeeded in overwhelming Peshmerga positions and seizing the towns of Mala Abdullah, Maryam Beg, Tel Ward and the Maktab Khalid crossing. Parts of the Khabbaz oil fields were also captured, taking 24 workers hostage. At least 25 Peshmerga fighters died including Brig. Gen. Sherko Shwani, commander of the 1st Brigade and the highest ranking head of Peshmerga forces in Kirkuk. Gen.
paraphrase: The confrontation known as the Battle of Kirkuk occurred within the city limits of Kirkuk, located in northern Iraq, involving forces from Iraqi Kurdistan and the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant. During the evening hours of January 29, approximately 150 combatants from ISIL launched an assault on fortifications situated to the south and west of Kirkuk, Iraq, which were then being held by the Peshmerga. The ISIL-led assault commenced concealed by a thick fog, allowing them to overpower the Peshmerga strongholds and gain control of the towns of Mala Abdullah, Maryam Beg, Tel Ward, and the Maktab Khalid crossing. Additionally, segments of the Khabbaz oil fields were seized, resulting in the abduction of 24 laborers. A minimum of 25 Peshmerga combatants lost their lives, among them Brigadier General Sherko Shwani, who was the commander of the 1st Brigade and the most senior-ranking officer of Peshmerga forces stationed in Kirkuk.

original: The 2015 FIBA Asia Championship was the 28th and last edition of the FIBA Asia Championship in men's basketball in Asia. It was organised by FIBA Asia. At the FIBA Asia Congress held in Doha, Qatar, the Central Board awarded the hosting rights to China. On 4 December 2014, the Chinese Basketball Association acquired approval from FIBA Asia to hold the men's championship at Changsha, Hunan, with the women's championship to be held at Wuhan, Hubei. As the winner of the tournament, China qualified for the basketball tournament at the 2016 Summer Olympics, while the Philippines, Iran, and Japan would participate at the 2016 FIBA World Olympic Qualifying Tournament for Men, as stated by FIBA and the IOC in qualification quota. original: On 26 March 2024, in Bisham Tehsil, Shangla District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan, a suicide bomber attacked a bus transporting five Chinese engineers and their Pakistani driver on their way to the Dasu Dam, killing all of them. The bomber rammed an explosive-filled car into the bus that was travelling from Islamabad to Kohistan on the Karakoram Highway. No group has taken responsibility for the attack. The Taliban-led government of Afghanistan also rejects Afghan involvement in the attack. This was the third attack on Chinese interests in Pakistan in the past week, as a Pakistani naval base and a port used by China were also attacked by Baloch separatists. The Chinese Embassy in Pakistan told Chinese nationals in Pakistan to stay alert. Aftab Khan Sherpao, Central Chairman of Qaumi
paraphrase: On March 26, 2024, a suicide bomber carried out an assault in Bisham Tehsil, located in the Shangla District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan. The assailant targeted a bus that was carrying five Chinese engineers along with their Pakistani driver as they journeyed to the Dasu Dam, resulting in the fatalities of all individuals aboard. The attacker executed the strike by crashing a vehicle laden with explosives into the bus, which was traveling along the Karakoram Highway, making its way from Islamabad to Kohistan. As of yet, no organization has come forward to claim responsibility for this act. Furthermore, the Taliban-governed administration in Afghanistan has denied any Afghan participation in the incident. This particular event marked the third aggression directed at Chinese assets in Pakistan within a single week, following previous assaults by Baloch separatists on a Pakistani naval base and a port utilized by China. In response, the Chinese Embassy in Pakistan issued a warning to Chinese citizens residing in Pakistan, advising them to remain vigilant. Aftab Khan Sherpao, who serves as the Central Chairman of Qaumi, was among those who commented on the situation.

original: The 2024 World Para Athletics Championships was a Paralympic track and field meet organized by the World Para Athletics, the respective sport branch of the International Paralympic Committee. This was the 11th edition of the event and was held at Kobe Universiade Memorial Stadium in Japan, from 17 to 25 May 2024. This was the first time the event was held in East Asia. The event was initially scheduled for September 2021 but it was rescheduled to avoid clashing with the 2020 Summer Paralympics which were rescheduled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In January 2022, the organisers requested World Para Athletics for postponement of the event until 2024 due to COVID-19 concerns. A week later, World Para Athletics confirmed that the event would not be held in 2022.

This particular iteration marked the eleventh occasion of the event, which took place in Japan at the Kobe Universiade Memorial Stadium, spanning from May 17th to May 25th, 2024. Notably, this represented the inaugural instance of the event being hosted in the East Asian region. Initially, the competition was slated for September 2021; however, its timing was subsequently adjusted to prevent any overlap with the 2020 Summer Paralympics, which themselves had been postponed as a consequence of the COVID-19 global health crisis. In January 2022, the entities responsible for organizing the championships formally requested World Para Athletics to defer the event until 2024, citing ongoing concerns related to COVID-19. Subsequently, within a week of this request, World Para Athletics officially declared that the event would not proceed in the year 2022 as previously considered.

original: The Böhmermann affair (also known as Erdogate) was a political affair following an experimental poem on German satirist Jan Böhmermann's satire show Neo Magazin Royale in late March 2016 that deliberately insulted Turkish president Recep Tayyip Erdoğan using profane language. paraphrase: The Böhmermann incident, also referred to as Erdogate, emerged as a political controversy subsequent to the broadcast of an experimental poem during the German satirist Jan Böhmermann's program, Neo Magazin Royale, in the latter part of March 2016. This poem intentionally directed insults toward Turkish president Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, employing offensive language. The affair unfolded mere days after a musical video, "Erdowie, Erdowo, Erdoğan," featured on a separate German satirical show, had provoked Erdoğan's anger, leading Ankara to summon the German ambassador. Böhmermann subsequently embarked on an exploration of the boundary separating satire, which enjoys protection under freedom of speech statutes, and "abusive criticism" (known in German as Schmähkritik) directed at a foreign head of state (an act akin to lèse-majesté), which, at that time, still constituted a prosecutable offense in Germany. With explicit acknowledgment that this endeavor was intentionally provocative and "prohibited," Böhmermann proceeded to recite a poem that went beyond merely criticizing Erdoğan in a severe manner, instead employing highly inflammatory rhetoric to challenge the established norms of comedic expression.

original: The 2015 Indianapolis motorcycle Grand Prix was the tenth round of the 2015 Grand Prix motorcycle racing season. It was held at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway in Speedway, Indiana on 9 August 2015. In the MotoGP category, Marc Márquez took his fifth pole position of the season and ultimately took his third win of the season — and his fifth consecutive win at Indianapolis Motor Speedway. Márquez's victory was also the 700th victory in the Grand Prix history for the Honda brand. Behind Márquez were the two Movistar Yamaha riders, Jorge Lorenzo, and Valentino Rossi; the podium was swept by these riders in the previous year. Further down the order, Ducati's Andrea Dovizioso started from tenth on the grid, but he went off track on the first lap.

This particular flight was operated by Germanwings, a budget airline that was a subsidiary of the German air carrier Lufthansa. On March 24, 2015, the Airbus A320-211 aircraft involved in the incident tragically crashed approximately 100 kilometers (62 miles; 54 nautical miles) north-west of Nice, within the French Alps, resulting in the loss of all 150 individuals on board. The cause of the crash was intentionally orchestrated by the co-pilot, Andreas Lubitz. Lubitz had a history of receiving treatment for suicidal tendencies and had been deemed medically unfit for duty by his physician. Despite this, Lubitz concealed this critical information from his employer and proceeded to report for work. Shortly after the aircraft attained its cruising altitude and during a period when the captain had temporarily exited the cockpit, Lubitz intentionally secured the cockpit door and commenced a deliberate, controlled descent which persisted until the aircraft's impact.

This annual fixture, which began as the Presidential Cup in 1966, pits the reigning champions of the premier league against the victors of the preceding season's cup competition. In instances where the same club secures both the league title and the cup, the cup runner-up is granted the opportunity to participate. Nevertheless, the event did not unfold as initially organized due to unresolved conflicts between the participating clubs and the Saudi authorities. In the wake of these complications, the Turkish Football Federation subsequently declared that the postponed match would be held on April 7.

original: On July 1, 2015, 32-year-old Kathryn "Kate" Steinle was shot and killed while walking with her father and a friend along Pier 14 in the Embarcadero district of San Francisco. She was hit in the back by a single bullet. The man who fired the gun, José Inez García Zárate, said he had found it moments before, wrapped in cloth beneath a bench on which he was sitting, and that when he picked it up the weapon went off. The shot ricocheted off the concrete deck of the pier and struck the victim, who was about 90 feet (27m) away. Steinle died two hours later in a hospital as a result of her injuries. original: Tears of a Clown was a show by American singer Madonna, first held at the Forum Theatre in Melbourne, Australia on March 10, 2016. The singer had not included Australia during her previous five concert tours, until the Rebel Heart Tour (2015–2016), so she created the show for her Australian fans since they had waited so long for her to perform there. Madonna explained that the idea behind Tears of a Clown was to combine music and storytelling, being influenced by clowns. Tickets for the first show were made available to members of Madonna's official fan club, Icon, and were non-transferable, with the person's name printed on them. The first show of Tears of a Clown started four hours late, with the delay being caused by Madonna rehearsing.
paraphrase: The American vocalist, Madonna, debuted a performance titled "Tears of a Clown" on March 10, 2016, at the Forum Theatre in Melbourne, Australia. Prior to her Rebel Heart Tour (2015–2016), Madonna had excluded Australia from her preceding five concert tours. Consequently, she conceived this particular show as a gesture for her Australian admirers, acknowledging their extended anticipation for her live performance in their country. Madonna articulated that the core concept of "Tears of a Clown" involved fusing musical elements with narrative, drawing inspiration from clown performance. Access to tickets for the inaugural show was granted exclusively to individuals holding membership in Icon, Madonna's official fan club. These tickets were explicitly designated as non-transferable, and each ticket bore the purchaser's name. The premiere of "Tears of a Clown" commenced with a four-hour delay, a postponement attributed to Madonna's ongoing rehearsals.

original: The Manbij offensive, code-named Operation Martyr and Commander Faysal Abu Layla by the SDF, was a 2016 military offensive operation by the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) to capture the city of Manbij from the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL), and eventually, the ISIL-held areas through Al-Bab to Herbel, in the area referred to as the "Manbij Pocket" in the northern Aleppo Governorate. paraphrase: The Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) initiated a military campaign in 2016 known as the Manbij offensive, which they code-named Operation Martyr and Commander Faysal Abu Layla. The objective of this operation was to seize control of the city of Manbij from the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL). Furthermore, the SDF aimed to ultimately capture all ISIL-controlled territories stretching from Al-Bab to Herbel, a region situated within the northern Aleppo Governorate, commonly referred to as the "Manbij Pocket." A primary strategic imperative of this offensive was to sever the final supply lines utilized by ISIL that originated from Turkey. Additionally, the operation sought to hinder ISIL combatants from fleeing across the frontier shared by Syria and Turkey. During the initial five-day period of the offensive, the coalition led by the United States carried out more than 55 airstrikes, providing crucial support to the SDF's efforts. Following the successful capture of Manbij city on August 12, the SDF publicly declared their intention to persist with the offensive until its full conclusion.

original: On 12 April 2015, 24-year-old nursing student Karen Buckley was murdered in Glasgow, Scotland, after leaving a nightclub that she had attended with her friends at 1 a.m. She was approached by Alexander Pacteau, a 21-year-old courier company owner, and entered his car after presumably being offered a lift home. Pacteau went on to beat her to death with a spanner, put her body inside a barrel full of caustic soda, and hide it at a farm shortly after. Only three days later, on 15 April 2015, Pacteau was arrested as a suspect in Buckley's disappearance after an abundance of evidence pointed to him being potentially guilty. He would later confess to her murder and be sent to life imprisonment with the possibility of parole in 23 years.
paraphrase: On April 12, 2015, Karen Buckley, a 24-year-old student nurse, was tragically killed in Glasgow, Scotland. This occurred subsequent to her departure from a nightclub, which she had been visiting with her companions around 1:00 a.m. Alexander Pacteau, a 21-year-old proprietor of a courier business, approached her, and it is presumed she accepted a ride home in his vehicle. Soon thereafter, Pacteau proceeded to fatally assault her using a spanner, subsequently placing her remains into a barrel filled with caustic soda, and concealing it on a farm. A mere three days later, on April 15, 2015, Pacteau was apprehended in connection with Buckley's vanishing, following a substantial accumulation of evidence that indicated his potential culpability. He subsequently admitted his involvement in her murder and received a sentence of life imprisonment, with the prospect of parole after 23 years.

original: The 2024 United Kingdom local elections took place on 2 May 2024 to choose 2,658 councillors on 107 councils in England, 11 directly elected mayors in England, the 25 members of the London Assembly, and 37 police and crime commissioners in England and Wales. The 2024 Blackpool South parliamentary by-election was held on the same day. These local elections were the last set of routine elections before the general election. paraphrase: On May 2, 2024, local elections were conducted across the United Kingdom. These elections involved the selection of 2,658 councillors across 107 English councils, 11 mayors directly elected in England, all 25 members of the London Assembly, and 37 police and crime commissioners situated in both England and Wales. Coinciding with these local electoral events, the 2024 Blackpool South parliamentary by-election also occurred. Notably, this series of local elections represented the final regularly scheduled electoral contests preceding the upcoming general election. The four-year cycle for these elections was reinstated with this cycle, given that the preceding iteration of most of these elections took place in 2021, having been postponed by one year from 2020 because of the global COVID-19 pandemic. The outcomes indicated a significant performance by the Labour Party, which secured the top position, largely at the detriment of the incumbent Conservative Party, which experienced a less favorable result.

original: Snap legislative elections were held on 10 March 2024 to elect members of the Assembly of the Republic to the 16th Legislature of Portugal. All 230 seats to the Assembly of the Republic were up for election. The elections were called in November 2023 after Prime Minister António Costa's resignation following an investigation around alleged corruption involving the award of contracts for lithium and hydrogen businesses. No party achieved an absolute majority of seats, with the centre-right Democratic Alliance (AD) led by Luís Montenegro winning 80 seats, closely followed by the Socialist Party (PS), which lost the absolute majority it had gained in the 2022 elections, and was reduced to 78 seats. The election also saw the surge of the right-wing populist Chega party as the third-largest party
paraphrase: On March 10, 2024, an unscheduled legislative election took place in Portugal to select the individuals who would serve as members of the Assembly of the Republic for its 16th Legislature. Every single one of the 230 positions within the Assembly of the Republic was open for this electoral contest. The decision to hold these elections was made in November 2023, following the resignation of Prime Minister António Costa. His resignation occurred in the wake of an inquiry into accusations of corruption related to the awarding of contracts for ventures involving lithium and hydrogen. No single political party secured an outright majority of the available seats. The center-right Democratic Alliance (AD), under the leadership of Luís Montenegro, emerged victorious with 80 seats. Trailing closely behind was the Socialist Party (PS), which had previously attained an absolute majority in the 2022 elections but subsequently saw its representation diminish to 78 seats. A notable outcome of this election was the significant rise of the right-wing populist Chega party, which established itself as the third-largest political force.

original: On September 21, 2016, a boat capsized off the Egyptian coast with around 600 refugees on board in the Mediterranean Sea. 204 bodies were recovered (including at least 30 children), around 160 people were rescued, and hundreds of people remain missing, with approximately 300 people presumed dead. Four people were arrested for trafficking and breaking capacity laws. The incident was the worst in 2016 in the Mediterranean Sea. In March 2017, the BBC reported that 56 people were convicted and sentenced to prison relating to the capsizing. The longest sentence was 14 years. The charges ranged from murder, manslaughter and negligence to lesser charges of not using sufficient rescue equipment, endangering lives, receiving money from the victims, hiding suspects from authorities, and using a vessel without a license.
paraphrase: On September 21, 2016, a vessel overturned in the Mediterranean Sea, near the Egyptian coast, carrying an estimated 600 refugees. Following this tragic event, 204 bodies were found, among them at least 30 children, while approximately 160 individuals were saved. A significant number of people, believed to be around 300, are still unaccounted for and presumed to have perished. Authorities apprehended four individuals on charges related to human trafficking and violating capacity regulations. paraphrase: Following the victory of pro-independence political groups, who secured a majority of seats in the Catalan election held on September 27, 2015, the Declaration of the Initiation of the Process of Independence of Catalonia (known in Catalan as Declaració d'inici del procés d'independència de Catalunya) was subsequently enacted on November 9, 2015. This declaration formally announced the commencement of the endeavor to establish an autonomous Catalan state, structured as a republic. Furthermore, it heralded the inception of a constituent process that was characterized as participative, open, integrating, and active, involving citizens in laying the groundwork for the forthcoming Catalan Constitution. The Parliament of Catalonia adopted this declaration with 72 votes supporting it, 63 votes opposing it, and no abstentions. Later, on June 9, 2017, the Catalan government publicly disclosed the scheduled date for the independence referendum. However, on September 6, 2017, this referendum was deemed unlawful and consequently suspended.

original: General elections were held in Solomon Islands on 17 April 2024 to determine the composition of the 12th Parliament. Initially planned for 2023, parliament voted in 2022 to delay the elections. Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare claimed the country could not afford to have an election in the same year the Solomon Islands were hosting the Pacific Games. The opposition condemned the delay and accused Sogavare of a power grab. A major campaign issue included the country's ties with China; however, domestic issues were a primary concern amongst many voters, including the cost of living, the national debt and medicine shortages. Eight parties and ten independents won seats. Prime Minister Sogavare's Ownership, Unity and Responsibility Party (OUR) won the most seats, securing 15, but fell short of a majority.
paraphrase: On April 17, 2024, general elections took place in the Solomon Islands to elect members to the 12th Parliament. These elections were originally scheduled for 2023, but a parliamentary vote in 2022 resulted in their postponement. Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare justified this delay by stating that the nation lacked the financial capacity to hold elections simultaneously with its hosting of the Pacific Games. Conversely, the opposition strongly criticized this deferral, alleging that Sogavare was attempting to consolidate power. A significant point of discussion during the election campaign was the Solomon Islands' relationship with China. Nevertheless, many voters were primarily focused on internal matters, such as the rising cost of living, the substantial national debt, and scarcity of medications. In total, eight political parties and ten independent candidates secured seats. The Ownership, Unity and Responsibility Party (OUR), led by Prime Minister Sogavare, obtained the highest number of seats, with 15, yet it did not achieve a majority.

paraphrase: Christina Victoria Grimmie, born on March 12, 1994, and passing away on June 10, 2016, was an American vocalist and an online video creator on YouTube. Her journey began in 2009 when she started uploading renditions of well-known songs to her YouTube channel. Following the launch of her inaugural extended play (EP), titled *Find Me*, in 2011, her YouTube channel successfully garnered one million subscribers. Upon achieving the milestone of two million subscribers, she unveiled her first studio album, *With Love*, in 2013. Subsequently, Grimmie participated as a competitor in the sixth season of the television series *The Voice*, where she secured third place. During the show's finale, Adam Levine, who served as her mentor, declared his intention to sign her to his record label, 222 Records, irrespective of the final outcome. Additionally, Lil Wayne extended an offer to sign her to his own label, Young Money Entertainment. She was briefly affiliated with Island Records before her contract was terminated. Grimmie proceeded to release her sophomore EP, *Side A*, in 2016.

original: The February 2015 North American cold wave was an extreme weather event that affected most of Canada and the eastern half of the United States. Following an earlier cold wave in the winter, the period of below-average temperatures contributed to an already unusually cold winter for the Eastern U.S. Several places broke their records for their coldest February on record, while some areas came very close. The cause of the cold wave was due to the polar vortex advancing southward into the eastern parts of the U.S, and even making it as far south as the Southeast, where large snow falls are rare. By the beginning of March, although the pattern did continue for the first week, it abated and retreated near the official end of the winter.
paraphrase: The North American continent experienced a severe cold wave in February 2015, which had a significant impact on the majority of Canada and the entire eastern portion of the United States. This period of exceptionally low temperatures, which occurred after a preceding cold wave earlier in the winter season, exacerbated what was already an uncharacteristically frigid winter for the Eastern U.S. Many locations registered their lowest February temperatures ever recorded, with a few others narrowly missing new records. The primary reason for this cold wave was the southerly progression of the polar vortex, which extended into the eastern regions of the U.S., reaching as far south as the Southeast, a region where substantial snow accumulation is typically uncommon. While the weather pattern persisted for the initial week of March, it gradually diminished and receded as the official end of winter approached.

original: On 15 March 2015, two explosions took place at Roman Catholic Church and Christ Church during Sunday service in Youhanabad, Lahore, Pakistan. At least 15 people were killed and seventy were wounded in the attacks. The attack was followed by mob violence killing two men mistaken as militants. It was the second attack on Christians minority after the deadliest attack in 2013 on All Saints church in Peshawar. In August 2015, Punjab home minister Shuja Khanzada announced that five members of the Tehreek-e-Taliban were arrested in connection to the attack. According to the statement, the men received monetary and material support from India and planned the attack in Afghanistan, from where they came to Lahore. The arrested men also confessed to planning further attacks in Karachi and Lahore.

Furthermore, the detained men admitted to having plotted additional attacks in both Karachi and Lahore.

This event was held on December 16, 2015, at the Arena Naucalpan, situated in Naucalpan, State of Mexico, Mexico. paraphrase: On June 9, 2024, Germany conducted its European Parliament election. This event marked the tenth parliamentary election since direct elections commenced in 1979, and it was also the inaugural European Parliament election to take place following Brexit. In the election, the CDU/CSU experienced a modest uptick in its share of the vote. Conversely, each of the three parties forming the government – the SPD, the Greens, and the FDP – garnered fewer votes than they did five years prior, with the Greens facing particularly substantial decreases. In stark contrast, the far-right AfD made considerable gains in both its vote count and the number of seats it secured, ultimately placing second. A notable regional imbalance was observed: the AfD achieved at least a plurality in every district of former East Germany, with the exception of six specific districts: Potsdam and Potsdam-Mittelmark within Brandenburg, the cities of Erfurt, Jena, and Weimar, and the historically Catholic region of Eichsfeld.

This Legislative Assembly is composed of 175 representatives, who are chosen through a system of universal adult suffrage, utilizing the first-past-the-post electoral method. The Legislative Assembly established its presence in the temporary Legislative Assembly Building located in Amaravati, starting with the Budget session of 2016. The newly constructed building is equipped with various systems designed for its operational needs.

original: The 2016 Kumamoto earthquakes (Japanese: 平成28年熊本地震, Hepburn: Heisei 28-nen Kumamoto jishin) were a series of earthquakes, including a magnitude 7.0 mainshock which struck at 01:25 JST on April 16, 2016 (16:25 UTC on April 15) beneath Kumamoto City of Kumamoto Prefecture in Kyushu Region, Japan, at a depth of about 10 kilometres (6.2 mi), and a foreshock earthquake with a magnitude 6.2 at 21:26 JST (12:26 UTC) on April 14, 2016, at a depth of about 11 kilometres (6.8 mi). In total, the two earthquakes killed 273 people and injured 2,809 others, a majority of them indirectly, making this the deadliest earthquake sequence in Japan since the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami. Of these, 50 deaths were directly caused by the earthquake, 218 were disaster-related deaths (災害関連死, Saigai-kanren-shi)
paraphrase: A succession of seismic events, known as the 2016 Kumamoto earthquakes (in Japanese: 平成28年熊本地震, Hepburn: Heisei 28-nen Kumamoto jishin), impacted the Kyushu Region of Japan. This sequence featured a significant mainshock, registered at a magnitude of 7.0, which occurred at 01:25 JST on April 16, 2016 (corresponding to 16:25 UTC on April 15). This powerful tremor originated approximately 10 kilometers (or 6.2 miles) below the surface, directly beneath Kumamoto City within Kumamoto Prefecture. Preceding this main event was a foreshock, which registered a magnitude of 6.2. This initial earthquake struck at 21:26 JST (or 12:26 UTC) on April 14, 2016, from a depth of approximately 11 kilometers (or 6.8 miles). The cumulative impact of these two seismic events resulted in a tragic loss of 273 lives and left 2,809 individuals with injuries. A substantial portion of these fatalities and injuries were indirectly attributable to the disaster. This series of earthquakes stands as the most lethal seismic sequence to affect Japan since the catastrophic 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami. Specifically, 50 of the reported deaths were a direct consequence of the earthquake's immediate effects, while an additional 218 fatalities were classified as disaster-related deaths (災害関連死, Saigai-kanren-shi).

original: This page documents all tornadoes confirmed by various weather forecast offices of the National Weather Service in the United States in June and July 2024. On average, there are 213 confirmed tornadoes in June and 119 confirmed tornadoes in July. In June, tornadoes are commonly focused across the Midwest and the central and northern Great Plains, and occasionally the Northeast, all due to their proximity to the late spring/early summer jet stream which continues to retreat farther north. Additionally, activity can sometimes increase in the Florida Peninsula as a result of early-season tropical activity. In July, the northern states nearer the Canadian border are most favored for tornadoes, including the Upper Midwest, the Great Lakes and the Northeastern states, due to the positioning of the summertime jet stream.

Typically, June sees an average of 213 confirmed tornadoes, while July averages 119 confirmed tornadoes. Throughout June, tornadoes tend to be concentrated in the Midwest, as well as the central and northern Great Plains, with sporadic occurrences in the Northeast. This pattern is attributed to these regions' closeness to the late spring/early summer jet stream, which progressively shifts northward. Furthermore, the Florida Peninsula may experience an increase in activity, occasionally, due to the presence of early-season tropical weather. By July, the northern states, particularly those near the Canadian border, are most susceptible to tornadoes, encompassing areas such as the Upper Midwest, the Great Lakes, and the Northeastern states, owing to the specific location of the summertime jet stream.

original: A series of ongoing protests at the Ohio State University by pro-Palestinian demonstrators have occurred on-campus in response to the Israel-Palestine conflict beginning on October 7, 2023. A solidarity encampment was constructed on OSU's South Oval on April 25, 2024, during which there were 36 arrests. About 40 protesters have been arrested across multiple non-violent protests, making for the largest en masse arrest on campus since the 1969–1970 Vietnam War protests. The protester demands of OSU include "financial divestment, academic boycott, financial disclosure, acknowledging the genocide, and ending targeted policing". Pro-Palestine groups have been critical of the university's response to the protests, suspension of pro-Palestinian student organizations, and suppression of the Undergraduate Student Government's attempts at passing legislation for financial divestment from Israel after receiving pressure from officials
paraphrase: At Ohio State University, a succession of continuous demonstrations by individuals supporting Palestine have been taking place on the university grounds. These demonstrations began following the onset of the Israel-Palestine conflict on October 7, 2023. On April 25, 2024, a solidarity encampment was established on OSU's South Oval, an event during which 36 individuals were taken into custody. Approximately 40 demonstrators have faced arrest across several non-violent protest actions, marking this as the most substantial mass arrest on the campus since the student protests against the Vietnam War from 1969 to 1970. The grievances articulated by the protesters directed at OSU encompass "financial divestment, an academic boycott, transparent financial disclosure, recognition of the genocide, and the cessation of targeted policing practices." Organizations advocating for Palestine have voiced strong disapproval regarding the university's handling of these protests, its decision to suspend pro-Palestinian student groups, and its interference with the Undergraduate Student Government's efforts to enact legislation aimed at divesting financially from Israel, reportedly due to pressure exerted by university officials.

original: Since 12 January 2024, the United States of America and the United Kingdom, with support from Australia, Bahrain, Canada, Denmark, the Netherlands, and New Zealand, have launched a series of cruise missile and airstrikes, codenamed Operation Poseidon Archer, against the Houthi movement (Ansar Allah) in Yemen in response to Houthi attacks on ships in the Red Sea. The Houthis had previously declared that their attacks are in support of Palestinians during the 2023 Israel–Hamas war; Houthi attacks on shipping were condemned by the United Nations Security Council the day before the initial strike. US President Joe Biden ordered the strikes, and UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak convened his cabinet to authorize British participation. American officials said the strikes were intended to degrade Houthi capabilities to attack Red Sea
paraphrase: Beginning on January 12, 2024, the United States of America and the United Kingdom, backed by Australia, Bahrain, Canada, Denmark, the Netherlands, and New Zealand, initiated a sequence of cruise missile and aerial assaults. These operations, given the codename Operation Poseidon Archer, targeted the Houthi movement, also known as Ansar Allah, within Yemen. This action was taken in retaliation for the Houthi's assaults on vessels navigating the Red Sea. Previously, the Houthis had stated that their aggression was in solidarity with Palestinians amidst the 2023 Israel–Hamas war. The day prior to the inaugural strike, the United Nations Security Council had denounced the Houthi's maritime attacks. US President Joe Biden gave the command for these strikes, while UK Prime Minister Rishi Sunak assembled his cabinet to give the green light for British involvement. According to American authorities, the objective of these strikes was to diminish the Houthi's capacity to launch attacks in the Red Sea.

original: The 2024 Championship League (officially the 2024 BetVictor Championship League) was a professional ranking snooker tournament that took place from 10 June to 3 July 2024 at the Leicester Arena in Leicester, England. The event featured 128 players and was played as three rounds of round‑robin groups of four, before a best‑of‑five final. It was the fifth edition of the ranking version of the Championship League, and the first ranking tournament of the 2024‍–‍25 season. Shaun Murphy was the defending champion, having defeated Mark Williams 3‍–‍0 in the final of the previous ranking edition of the tournament, but he was knocked out in Stage 2. Ali Carter won his sixth ranking title, defeating Jackson Page 3‍–‍1 in the final. There were 95 century breaks made during the tournament,
paraphrase: The 2024 Championship League, formally known as the 2024 BetVictor Championship League, constituted a professional snooker tournament with ranking status. This competition unfolded from June 10th to July 3rd, 2024, at the Leicester Arena, situated in Leicester, England. A total of 128 players participated in the event. The tournament format involved three stages of round-robin groups, each comprising four competitors, culminating in a best-of-five final match. This particular iteration marked the fifth occasion the Championship League had been designated as a ranking event, and it served as the inaugural ranking tournament of the 2024–25 snooker season. Shaun Murphy, who had claimed victory in the preceding ranking edition of the tournament by defeating Mark Williams 3–0 in the final, entered as the reigning champion; however, his participation concluded in Stage 2. Ultimately, Ali Carter secured his sixth career ranking title, triumphing over Jackson Page with a score of 3–1 in the final match. Throughout the duration of the tournament, a total of 95 century breaks were recorded.

original: Miss Grand Thailand 2024 (Thai: มิสแกรนด์ไทยแลนด์ 2024) was the 11th edition of the Miss Grand Thailand pageant, held on April 6, 2024, at the organizer-owned hall, MGI Hall, located in the Bravo BKK Mall, Bangkok. Representatives of seventy-seven administrative divisions of the country competed for the title, of whom a 27-year-old Thai-Singaporean beauty influencer and entrepreneur representing Phuket, Malin Chara-anan, was announced the winner. Malin was crowned by Miss Grand Thailand 2023, Thaweeporn Phingchamrat of Chumphon, and will represent Thailand at the international pageant, Miss Grand International 2024, to be held on 25 October 2024 in Myanmar. paraphrase: The eleventh iteration of the Miss Grand Thailand competition, known in Thai as มิสแกรนด์ไทยแลนด์ 2024, occurred on April 6, 2024. This event took place at the MGI Hall, a venue owned by the organizing body and situated within the Bravo BKK Mall in Bangkok. Contesting for the prestigious title were representatives from the seventy-seven distinct administrative regions of the nation. Ultimately, Malin Chara-anan, a 27-year-old beauty influencer and entrepreneur of Thai-Singaporean heritage, who represented Phuket, was declared the victor. Malin received her crown from Thaweeporn Phingchamrat of Chumphon, the reigning Miss Grand Thailand 2023. Malin is now set to represent Thailand in the global competition, Miss Grand International 2024, which is scheduled for October 25, 2024, in Myanmar. The initial plans for the pageant's boot camp involved holding it in Songkhla Province; however, these arrangements were subsequently withdrawn owing to a disagreement stemming from conflicting political ideologies between the national organizers and the royalist faction.

original: On 23 June 2024, coordinated attacks were launched in the cities of Derbent and Makhachkala in the Russian republic of Dagestan in the North Caucasus. Two synagogues, two Eastern Orthodox churches, and a traffic police post were attacked simultaneously with automatic weapons and Molotov cocktails. It was reported that 17 police officers and five civilians were killed along with all five attackers. The fatalities included a priest, Nikolay Kotelnikov. The Kele-Numaz Synagogue was nearly completely destroyed by fire in the attack. Russian authorities designated the attack as an act of terrorism. Russian media reported that five of the perpetrators were identified by authorities, including one son and two nephews of Magomed Omarov, the head of Dagestan's Sergokalinsky District. paraphrase: On June 23, 2024, a series of synchronized assaults took place across the cities of Derbent and Makhachkala, situated within the Russian republic of Dagestan in the North Caucasus region. These attacks involved the simultaneous targeting of two synagogues, two Eastern Orthodox churches, and a traffic police checkpoint, utilizing automatic firearms and incendiary devices such as Molotov cocktails. Accounts indicated that 17 police personnel and five non-combatants lost their lives, alongside all five individuals who carried out the attacks. Among those who perished was a clergyman named Nikolay Kotelnikov. The Kele-Numaz Synagogue suffered extensive damage, being almost entirely consumed by fire during the incident. Officials in Russia characterized these events as an act of terrorism. Reports from Russian media outlets revealed that authorities had identified five of the individuals responsible, among whom were a son and two nephews of Magomed Omarov, who held the position of head of Dagestan's Sergokalinsky District. Subsequently, Omarov tendered his resignation and was taken into custody.

original: The 2024 Trooping the Colour ceremony was held on Saturday 15 June to celebrate the official birthday of King Charles III. Apart from being Charles' second Trooping the Colour ceremony since his accession to the throne, the 2024 ceremony occurred shortly after he had returned to his official duties after receiving a cancer diagnosis, and while his treatment was ongoing. Buckingham Palace confirmed on 30 May that Charles would attend the ceremony, but that he would inspect troops while seated in an Ascot landau carriage alongside Queen Camilla rather than the traditional inspection done on horseback. It was also announced that the Princess of Wales's role as Inspecting Officer in the Colonel's Review, a parade that takes place a week before the Trooping the Colour ceremony, would be
paraphrase: The Trooping the Colour ceremony in 2024 took place on Saturday, June 15th, marking the official birthday of King Charles III. This particular event was notable for several reasons: it was the second Trooping the Colour King Charles III had experienced since becoming monarch, and it occurred soon after he had resumed his royal responsibilities following a cancer diagnosis, with his medical treatment still in progress. On May 30th, Buckingham Palace verified that King Charles III would be present at the ceremony. However, instead of performing the customary inspection of troops from horseback, he would conduct it from an Ascot landau carriage, accompanied by Queen Camilla. Furthermore, it was publicly stated that the Princess of Wales would not fulfill her duties as Inspecting Officer during the Colonel's Review, which is a parade conducted one week prior to the main Trooping the Colour ceremony.

original: In 2023 and 2024, a series of street demonstrations took place throughout Georgia largely in opposition to the proposed "Law on Transparency of Foreign Influence", which would require non-governmental organizations (NGOs) to register as foreign agents or "organizations carrying the interests of a foreign power" and disclose the sources of their income if the funds they receive from abroad amount to more than 20% of their total revenue. Protesters and other critics, including the European Union and other Western countries, have spoken out against the bill, holding that it will stifle democracy and freedom of press in Georgia and comparing it to the Russian foreign agent law. Proponents of the bill deny this characterization and say the bill will ensure transparency of foreign funding and protect Georgia's sovereignty
paraphrase: During 2023 and 2024, a sequence of public demonstrations occurred across Georgia, primarily in protest against the suggested "Law on Transparency of Foreign Influence." This legislative proposal mandated that non-governmental organizations (NGOs) would need to officially register either as foreign agents or as "organizations carrying the interests of a foreign power," and also divulge their income sources, particularly if the financial contributions they receive from international entities exceed 20% of their overall earnings. Both demonstrators and other detractors, among them the European Union and various Western nations, have voiced their disapproval of this legislation. They argue that its implementation would suppress democratic processes and journalistic freedoms within Georgia, frequently drawing parallels to Russia's foreign agent law. Conversely, those who support the bill reject these portrayals, asserting that the legislation is intended to guarantee clarity regarding foreign funding and to safeguard Georgia's national sovereignty.

paraphrase: The 2016 NCAA Division I men's basketball championship featured 68 participating teams competing in a single-elimination bracket format. The objective was to crown the men's National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I college basketball national champion for the 2015–16 athletic season. This iteration, the 78th occurrence of the Tournament, commenced on March 15, 2016, and culminated with the championship final held on April 4 at NRG Stadium in Houston, Texas. Notably, this particular NCAA tournament marked the inaugural adoption of the NCAA March Madness branding, which encompassed entirely branded court designs at every location hosting tournament games. A significant narrative unfolding during the Tournament's opening round revolved around numerous upsets; the most prominent of these saw the No. 15 seed Middle Tennessee triumph over the No. 2 seed Michigan State. This victory represented only the eighth instance in history where a No. 15 seed successfully defeated a No. 2 seed, marking it as the most substantial upset of the round. During this initial stage, at least one team that was seeded lower than its opponent managed to secure a victory.

paraphrase: The 27th gathering of the heads of government belonging to the Commonwealth of Nations, officially referred to as the 2024 Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting or CHOGM 2024, is slated to take place in Samoa. This significant event is planned for the period of October 21st to October 25th, 2024. The central focus for this year's meeting is encapsulated in the theme: "One Resilient Common Future: Transforming our Common Wealth." This theme aims to reinforce robust democratic frameworks that champion human rights, uphold democratic principles, and ensure the rule of law. Furthermore, a key objective is to address the critical issue of climate change, with a particular emphasis on its detrimental effects on smaller nations and island states. Additionally, it will represent the first Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting ever to be hosted in a Pacific small island developing state.

original: Miss Grand United States Virgin Islands 2024 was the inaugural edition of the Miss Grand United States Virgin Islands pageant, held on March 25, 2024, at the CUZ Miami Venue, Doral, Florida, United States. paraphrase: On May 3, 2015, a thwarted terrorist attack took place at the Curtis Culwell Center in Garland, Texas, US. The target was an exhibition showcasing cartoon depictions of Muhammad. The incident culminated in a gunfight with law enforcement personnel who were providing security for the event, resulting in the fatalities of the two individuals who carried out the attack. original: The Soberanes Fire was a large wildfire that burned from July to October 2016 in the Santa Lucia Mountains of Monterey County, California. It destroyed 57 homes and killed a bulldozer operator, and cost about $260 million to suppress, making it at the time the most expensive wildfire to fight in United States history. At the fire's peak, over 5,000 personnel were assigned to the blaze. The fire was the result of an illegal campfire in Garrapata State Park. paraphrase: The Soberanes Fire, a substantial wildfire, raged from July through October 2016 within California's Santa Lucia Mountains, specifically in Monterey County. It was responsible for the destruction of 57 residences and the fatality of a bulldozer operator. The cost associated with its suppression amounted to approximately $260 million, establishing it as the most costly wildfire to combat in the history of the United States at that time. During its most intense period, more than 5,000 individuals were deployed to manage the conflagration. The origin of the fire was traced back to an unlawful campfire situated within Garrapata State Park. Before its complete extinguishment, the inferno had consumed an area of 132,127 acres (53,470 ha) situated along the Big Sur coastline, encompassing the Los Padres National Forest, Ventana Wilderness, and neighboring private and public properties in Monterey County, thereby securing its position as the 18th largest wildfire in California's recorded history.

original: The 2019–2024 Amaravati protests, simply known as Amaravati protests, were demonstrations in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh triggered by the idea of changing the one capital of Amaravati already identified and developed partially, to three capitals of Amaravati, Visakhapatnam and Kurnool by the Government of Andhra Pradesh. This led to concerns that the decision would create chaos and insecurity for farmers who gave their fertile agricultural lands to the government in 29 villages of Guntur district. The protests began in Mandadam, Thullur, Uddandarayunipalem on 18 December 2019. In a few days, the protests spread across Andhra Pradesh Capital Region, in Andhra Pradesh. On 17 December 2020, series of events were done to mark the protests' anniversary. The 1,631-days long protests were ended on 12 June 2024. The
paraphrase: The Amaravati protests, which took place from 2019 to 2024, constituted a series of demonstrations within the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. These protests were initiated in response to the Andhra Pradesh Government's proposal to alter the existing single capital of Amaravati, which had already been partially identified and developed, into a system of three capitals: Amaravati, Visakhapatnam, and Kurnool. This proposition generated significant apprehension, particularly among farmers who had ceded their productive agricultural lands to the government across 29 villages in the Guntur district, as they feared the decision would lead to disorder and instability. The initial protests commenced on 18 December 2019, specifically in the locations of Mandadam, Thullur, and Uddandarayunipalem. Within a short period, the demonstrations expanded their reach throughout the Andhra Pradesh Capital Region, situated within Andhra Pradesh itself. A year later, on 17 December 2020, various commemorative events were organized to observe the anniversary of the protests' inception. These extensive protests, spanning a duration of 1,631 days, concluded on 12 June 2024.

original: On 7 March 2015, a steam-hauled charter train passed a signal at danger and subsequently came to a stand across a high-speed mainline junction near Wootton Bassett Junction, Wiltshire, England. Another train, which had right of way, had passed through the junction 44 seconds earlier and no collision occurred nor was any damage done. As a result of this signal passed at danger (SPAD), Network Rail banned the train's operator, West Coast Railway Company (WCRC), from operating trains anywhere on the British railway network. The Rail Accident Investigation Branch (RAIB) opened an investigation into the incident, which it called a "dangerous occurrence". The organization known as the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) took credit for orchestrating these assaults. These bombings represented the most severe act of terrorism experienced in Beirut since the conclusion of the Lebanese Civil War. The incident occurred twelve days following the bombing of a Russian passenger aircraft above the Sinai Peninsula, which resulted in the deaths of 224 individuals, and one day prior to a series of attacks in Paris that claimed 137 lives. ISIL also asserted responsibility for these subsequent attacks. Approximately 48 hours subsequent to the Bourj el-Barajneh attack, the Internal Security Forces apprehended eleven individuals, predominantly Syrian nationals, in connection with the incident. The authorities subsequently made an announcement regarding this.

original: The 2015 Kharkiv bombing occurred on 22 February 2015, when a bomb hit a Ukrainian national unity rally in the Ukrainian city of Kharkiv, Kharkiv Oblast. The blast killed at least three people and injured another 10, including a boy age 15 and a policeman. On 25 February the death toll rose to four. It was one of many bombings in the cities of the Kharkiv oblast and Odesa oblast. Security forces arrested four people after the attack. More attacks happened in the city afterwards. Bloomberg reported that the deadly attack in the government-controlled city assisted the decline of the hryvnia, Ukraine's national currency. On 28 December 2019 suspects Viktor Tetyutsk, Serhiy Bashlykov and Volodymyr Dvornikov were sentenced to life imprisonment. They were released (during a major prison
paraphrase: On February 22, 2015, a bombing incident took place in Kharkiv, Ukraine. A bomb detonated at a Ukrainian national unity demonstration in Kharkiv, which is located in the Kharkiv Oblast. This explosion resulted in the immediate deaths of at least three individuals and caused injuries to ten others. Among the injured were a 15-year-old boy and a police officer. By February 25, the number of fatalities had increased to four. This event was part of a series of bombings that affected both the Kharkiv and Odesa oblasts. Following the attack, law enforcement officials apprehended four suspects. Additional attacks subsequently occurred within the city. According to a report by Bloomberg, this fatal assault in the city, which was under government control, contributed to a depreciation of the hryvnia, the official currency of Ukraine. On December 28, 2019, Viktor Tetyutsk, Serhiy Bashlykov, and Volodymyr Dvornikov, who were identified as suspects, received life sentences. However, they were later released as part of a significant prisoner exchange.

original: The 37th Annual Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards ceremony was held on July 13, 2024, at the Barker Hangar in Santa Monica, California. It aired live on Nickelodeon and in a domestic simulcast with several other Paramount Global cable networks, and was broadcast live or tape delayed across all of Nickelodeon's international networks. The ceremony was dedicated to the 25th anniversary of SpongeBob SquarePants, with the ceremony itself hosted by none other than him and Patrick Star, Tom Kenny and Bill Fagerbakke reprising their roles, marking the first time the ceremony has been hosted by virtual hosts. original: The 2024 Nürburgring 24 Hours (officially known as ADAC Ravenol 24h Race at the Nürburgring Nordschleife for sponsorship reasons) was the 52nd running of the Nürburgring 24 Hours, which took place over 1–2 June 2024. Though still organised by the ADAC Nordrhein, the edition marked the start of a new partnership with the SRO Motorsports Group that saw the event stage the second round of the 2024 Intercontinental GT Challenge. This was the first race with lubricant manufacturer Ravenol as title sponsor, replacing TotalEnergies. The race was, at the time of completion, the shortest in the history of the event, with only 7 hours and 24 minutes of running before being red flagged due to excessive fog. The race was won by the #16 Scherer Sport PHX, tying
paraphrase: The 52nd iteration of the Nürburgring 24 Hours, officially designated as the ADAC Ravenol 24h Race at the Nürburgring Nordschleife due to sponsorship agreements, unfolded across the 1st and 2nd of June in 2024. While the ADAC Nordrhein maintained its role as the organizing body for this event, this particular running heralded the initiation of a fresh collaborative venture with the SRO Motorsports Group. This partnership led to the Nürburgring 24 Hours serving as the second competitive round within the 2024 Intercontinental GT Challenge series. Furthermore, this race represented a significant milestone as it was the inaugural event to feature the lubricant producer Ravenol as its primary title sponsor, taking over from the previous sponsor, TotalEnergies. Upon its conclusion, this specific race distinguished itself as the briefest in the entire history of the competition, having only been actively contested for a duration of 7 hours and 24 minutes before being suspended with a red flag due to an overwhelming prevalence of fog. The victor of this historically short race was the #16 Scherer Sport PHX team, thereby achieving a tie in the record books.

original: Parliamentary elections were held in Croatia on 17 April 2024 to elect the members of the 11th Sabor. Prior to the elections, the government consisted of a coalition of the Croatian Democratic Union and Independent Democratic Serb Party, with parliamentary support of five national minority MPs, two MPs from the Croatian Social Liberal Party and Croatian Demochristian Party, and one independent MP, Silvano Hrelja. The ruling centre-right HDZ won a plurality of seats for the fourth straight election obtaining a victory over the centre-left Rivers of Justice. The right-wing populist Homeland Movement finished third, with the left-wing and green We can! in fourth place. The HDZ subsequently formed a right-wing coalition with the Homeland Movement and returned HDZ leader Andrej Plenković as Prime Minister for a third term.
paraphrase: On April 17, 2024, Croatia conducted parliamentary elections to choose representatives for the 11th Sabor. Before these elections took place, the government was formed by a collaborative alliance comprising the Croatian Democratic Union and the Independent Democratic Serb Party. This governing body also received legislative backing from five members of parliament representing national minorities, two MPs from the Croatian Social Liberal Party and the Croatian Demochristian Party, and a single independent MP named Silvano Hrelja. In a significant outcome, the incumbent centre-right Croatian Democratic Union (HDZ) secured the largest number of seats for the fourth consecutive election, triumphing over the centre-left Rivers of Justice alliance. The right-wing populist Homeland Movement emerged as the third strongest force, while the left-wing and green party, We Can!, secured the fourth position. Following these results, the HDZ proceeded to establish a right-wing coalition government with the Homeland Movement, which led to Andrej Plenković, the leader of the HDZ, being reinstated as Prime Minister for his third term in office.

original: The 53rd New York Film Festival was held September 25 – October 11, 2015. The lineup consisted of seven sections: Main Slate (26 films and four shorts programs) Spotlight on Documentary (11 films and one shorts program) Projections (14 programs) Special Events (10 films) Revivals (11 films) Nathaniel Dorsky & Jerome Hiler Retrospective (12 programs) Convergence (transmedia presentations and talks) The Festival also included various talks and free screenings. The Dorsky/Hiler retrospective was programmed by Gavin Smith, and Convergence was curated by Matt
paraphrase:
The 53rd installment of the New York Film Festival took place from September 25th to October 11th in 2015. The festival's programming was organized into seven distinct categories: the Main Slate, which featured 26 motion pictures along with four collections of short films; Spotlight on Documentary, showcasing 11 documentary features and one collection of short films; Projections, presenting 14 different programs; Special Events, which included 10 films; Revivals, highlighting 11 cinematic works; a Retrospective dedicated to Nathaniel Dorsky & Jerome Hiler, comprising 12 programs; and Convergence, which involved transmedia presentations and discussions. Furthermore, the Festival offered a variety of discussions and complimentary film viewings.

The core committee responsible for film selection comprised Kent Jones, who served as chair, Dennis Lim, Marian Masone, Gavin Smith, and Amy Taubin. The selection of the Shorts Programs was handled by Sarah Mankoff, Laura Kern, Matt Bolish, Florence Almozini, and Dan Sullivan. The programming for Projections was carried out by Dennis Lim, Aily Nash, and Gavin Smith. Gavin Smith also curated the Dorsky/Hiler retrospective, while Matt Bolish was responsible for curating the Convergence section.

original: The Battle of Kirkuk took place in the city of Kirkuk in northern Iraq between Iraqi Kurdistan and the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant. On the night of January 29, around 150 ISIL fighters attacked positions south and west of the city of Kirkuk, Iraq, which were temporarily under the control of the Peshmerga. The ISIL offensive began under the cover of dense fog and succeeded in overwhelming Peshmerga positions and seizing the towns of Mala Abdullah, Maryam Beg, Tel Ward and the Maktab Khalid crossing. Parts of the Khabbaz oil fields were also captured, taking 24 workers hostage. At least 25 Peshmerga fighters died including Brig. Gen. Sherko Shwani, commander of the 1st Brigade and the highest ranking head of Peshmerga forces in Kirkuk. Gen.

original: The National Museum of Natural History (NMNH) was a museum focusing on nature, located in New Delhi, India. Established in 1972 and opened in 1978, the museum functioned under the Ministry of Environment and Forests of the Government of India. The museum was situated on Barakhamba Road at Tansen Marg in central New Delhi, across from the Embassy of Nepal, near the Connaught Place metro station. On 26 April 2016, the museum building and much of its collection were destroyed by a fire. paraphrase: On November 8, 2016, a special election was held in Oregon for the governorship. The primary purpose of this particular election was to select an individual to serve the concluding two years of the term previously held by Democratic governor John Kitzhaber. Kate Brown, the incumbent Democratic governor, who had assumed the governorship as the Oregon Secretary of State, sought election to the office. This seismic event originated at a depth of 210.4 kilometers (equivalent to 130.7 miles) beneath the Earth's surface. The intensity of this particular tremor reached a maximum level of V, which is categorized as "Moderate" on the Modified Mercalli Intensity scale.

Tragically, this earthquake resulted in the fatalities of at least 5 individuals within Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, with an additional death recorded in Gilgit Baltistan. Furthermore, the seismic activity caused injuries to 46 people across these two provinces. original: The 2024 Belgian provincial, municipal and district elections will take place on Sunday 13 October 2024. They take place four months after the simultaneous European, federal and regional elections. The local elections are organised by the respective regions: Brussels with 19 municipalities Flanders with 5 provinces and 285 municipalities (down from 300) In the city of Antwerp, elections will also be held for its 10 districts (up from 9) Wallonia with 5 provinces and 261 municipalities (one fewer) In the German-speaking Community, the elections are organised by that community rather than the Walloon Region In the municipalities with language facilities of Voeren, Comines-Warneton and the 6 of the Brussels Periphery, the aldermen and members of the OCMW/CPAS council are directly elected. As reflected in the number of municipalities
paraphrase:
The upcoming 2024 Belgian provincial, municipal, and district elections are scheduled to occur on Sunday, October 13, 2024. These elections are set to happen four months subsequent to the concurrent European, federal, and regional elections. The organization of these local elections falls under the purview of their respective regions:

*   **Brussels:** This region includes 19 municipalities.
*   **Flanders:** This area encompasses 5 provinces and a total of 285 municipalities, a reduction from the previous 300. Within the city of Antwerp, elections will additionally be conducted for its 10 districts, an increase from the prior 9.
*   **Wallonia:** This region comprises 5 provinces and 261 municipalities, which is one fewer than before. Notably, within the German-speaking Community, these elections are managed directly by that community itself, rather than by the Walloon Region.

Furthermore, in the municipalities featuring language facilities, specifically Voeren, Comines-Warneton, and the 6 municipalities located in the Brussels Periphery, the aldermen and the members of the OCMW/CPAS council are chosen through direct election. This arrangement is also mirrored in the varying number of municipalities across the regions.

original: The 2019–2024 Amaravati protests, simply known as Amaravati protests, were demonstrations in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh triggered by the idea of changing the one capital of Amaravati already identified and developed partially, to three capitals of Amaravati, Visakhapatnam and Kurnool by the Government of Andhra Pradesh. This led to concerns that the decision would create chaos and insecurity for farmers who gave their fertile agricultural lands to the government in 29 villages of Guntur district. The protests began in Mandadam, Thullur, Uddandarayunipalem on 18 December 2019. In a few days, the protests spread across Andhra Pradesh Capital Region, in Andhra Pradesh. On 17 December 2020, series of events were done to mark the protests' anniversary. The 1,631-days long protests were ended on 12 June 2024. The
paraphrase: The Amaravati protests, which spanned from 2019 to 2024, constituted a series of demonstrations within the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. These protests were sparked by the Andhra Pradesh government's proposal to transition from a single capital, Amaravati – which had already been partially designated and developed – to a system of three capitals: Amaravati, Visakhapatnam, and Kurnool. This proposed change ignited apprehension among farmers, who feared that such a decision would lead to disorder and instability. These farmers had previously relinquished their productive agricultural lands to the government across 29 villages situated in the Guntur district. The initial outbreak of these protests occurred in Mandadam, Thullur, and Uddandarayunipalem on December 18, 2019. Within a short period, the unrest expanded throughout the Andhra Pradesh Capital Region, located in Andhra Pradesh. A year later, on December 17, 2020, various events were organized to commemorate the anniversary of the protests. The protests, which persisted for an extensive period of 1,631 days, ultimately concluded on June 12, 2024.

original: The Raqqa campaign (codenamed Operation Wrath of Euphrates) was a military operation launched in November 2016 during the Rojava–Islamist conflict by the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) against the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in the Raqqa Governorate, with the goal of isolating and eventually capturing the Islamic State's capital city, Raqqa. The SDF's subsidiary goals included capturing the Tabqa Dam, the nearby city of al-Thawrah, and the Baath Dam further downstream. The campaign ended successfully in October 2017, with the capture of Raqqa. The offensive was concurrent with the Battle of Sirte in Libya, the Battle of Mosul in Iraq, the Battle of al-Bab in the Aleppo Governorate, the Palmyra offensive, the reignition of fighting in Deir ez-Zor's siege, the Central Syria campaign, and the
paraphrase: Operation Wrath of Euphrates, also known as the Raqqa campaign, represented a military endeavor that commenced in November 2016. This operation was initiated by the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) as part of the broader Rojava–Islamist conflict, targeting the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) within the Raqqa Governorate. The primary objective of this undertaking was to encircle and subsequently seize Raqqa, which served as the capital city for the Islamic State. Beyond this central aim, the SDF also harbored additional objectives, including the capture of the Tabqa Dam, the adjacent city of al-Thawrah, and the Baath Dam, situated further downriver. The campaign concluded triumphantly in October 2017, culminating in the successful seizure of Raqqa. This offensive unfolded simultaneously with several other major military engagements, namely the Battle of Sirte in Libya, the Battle of Mosul in Iraq, the Battle of al-Bab within the Aleppo Governorate, the Palmyra offensive, the resurgence of hostilities during the siege of Deir ez-Zor, and the Central Syria campaign.

Specifically, the Conservatives successfully unseated a Labour representative in Brighton Hill North and also took two seats from independent candidates in the wards of Chineham and Tadley Central.
